example: Cit1-leh1 si33 wah4-eh3 a3dek1. (This is my younger brother.)
Ae33mui2 si33 mm33 si33 ti1 Teong3 Kork3? (Isn't Xiamen in China?)
Cui33-cui33 aet3 chiu4 ka1 lu4? (Who is waving at you? Dia melambaikan tangannya kepada saya.)

english: young man, addressing youths younger than yourself
example: Ah3 Ba2, lu1-eh3 Pa1 mia3 ha1mik1? (Boy, what's your father's name?)

english: uncle, form of address for men in their fifties and above
example: Wah1-eh3 Ah3 Cek3 hnua3 lor3li4. (My uncle drives lorry.)

english: uncle's wife, husband's younger brother's wife
example: Cit1-keng3 ok3tng2-eh3 hau3tniau4 si33 wah1-eh3 ah3 cim4 (The headmistress of this school is my uncle's wife.)

english: uncle, form of address for older men. Father's elder brother.
example: Cit1-leh1 Ah3 Pek3 si33 lu1-eh3 cui33-cui33? (How is this elderly man related to you?)
Wah1 beh4 du3rian2 hor33 wah1-eh3 Ah3 Tniau33. (I bought durians for my uncle.)

english: vain, excessively proud of or concerned about one's own appearance
example: Boh3lang3 su3kak3 ee1 eng3goay33 ee1 cin3 ai1bin33. (Nobody likes her because she is very vain.)

english: to want, to need (literally: to want it around)
example: Cit1-pun1 chaek3 wah4 ai1tni2 eh2. (I need this book.)
Wah1 ai3 cit1-leh1 mi3knia3. (I want this item.)
Uh3 cit3-khien1 ang3mor2 lai3 choay3 lu4. (There's a Westerner who came to look for you.)
Ang3mor3 iok1 khah1 eng3 ciak1. (It is easier to take Western medicine.)

english: vernacular term to mean Westerner, Caucasian, white person
example: Ee1 kau3in2 Ang3mor3 kau2. (She married a Westerner.)

english: Western-style bungalow, Literally: Westerner's building; 2. (figurative) bump on the head, especially after accidentally knocking oneself
example: Ie1-eh3 thau3khak3 kong33tiok3 piak3 liau3 snaeh3 ang3mor3lau2. (His head has a bump after accidentally hitting the wall.)
Haeh33 tam3pok3 ang3mor3tau33iu2 khah1 ho1ciak1. (Put a bit of Worcestershire sauce to make it taste better.)
Cit1-leh1 su33 pien1 cin3 ark1chak3. (This issue has become very messy.)
Ie1-eh3 cang2 si4 in3ui33 ie1 be33ki1 ark1 cui4. (His plants died because he forgot to water them.)
To3long3 chut3 khee1 ark1 cang2. (Please go out to water the plants.)

english: unreasonable, obstinate, stubbornly adhering to one's opinion
example: Ie1-eh3 hau3snaeh1 cin3 au1ban2. (His son is very obstinate.)
Ie au1hoek4 eng3goay33 am1seong1. (He vomitted blood due to internal injury.)
Ie1 khee1 khnua1 lo1kun1 eng3goay33 ie1 au1lau3. (He went to see the doctor because he has diarrhoea.)
Lu1 kong1 wa33 au1ta4 eh2. (Your words are absurd.)
Wah1-eh3 knia4 au4 eng3goay33 ie1 boh3 ho1si3. (My child vomitted because he wasn't feeling well.)

english: youth, lad, as in Ah3 Ba2
example: Heh1-leh1 siau1lien3 ba2 si33 cui33-cui33 lai2? (Who is that young man.)
Ee1 beh1 por3 snar3 ba33. (She bought three yards of cloth.)

english: welt, swelling caused by mosquito bite
example: Bang4 ka33 wah4 nor33 ba33. (I have two mosquite bites on me.)
Ie1 ban1tai4 ie1-eh3 knia4 in3ui33 thau3thek1 ie1-eh3 lui1. (He walloped his son for stealing his money.)
Ie1 co3 cit3-leh3 bang3cam3 beh33 mi3knia3. (He built a website to sell things.)
Cit1-leh1 bang33cam33 uh33 kae1-koay3 nor33-cheng3-leh3 bang33yak1. (This website has over two thousand pages.)
Wah4 mai1 kio33 ie1 co1 mi3knia3 in3ui33 ie1 be33 ha3rap3 eh2. (I won't ask him to do anything as he cannot be relied on.)
Ie1 thark3chaek3 ti1 Bee1 Kork3. (He studied in the United States.)

english: virtue, behaviour of a high moral standard, noble attribute

example: Today we eat Siamese rice.
Wah1 boh3 beng3peik1 cit1-leh1 boon3tay2. (I don't understand this problem.)
Lu1 boek3 ha1mik1? (What do you want?)
Ie1 boh3 ceng2-eh3 lang2. (He is an ungrateful person.)
Ie1-eh3 chia1 tiok3 long3 ta3pi1 ho1cai4 ie1 boh3 gai33 tiok1.
Boh3 kho1leng2 an1nae1 en3tau2-eh3 ta3por1 kokh1 boh3 ca3bor1 peng3iu4. (It is so improbable that such a handsome guy has no girlfriend.)
Wah1 boh3 kuan1si3 ciak3 cit1-leh1. (I am not familiar with eating this.)
Boh3 tnia3tiok1 gok3 ma1cai3 ie1 uh33 lai2 boh2. (It's still uncertain whether he would come tomorrow.)
Lu4 kao1giak3 boh3lor33yong3, a1nae1 kan1tan1 pun1 be3hiau1 co3. (You are really useless, so easy also cannot do.)
Ie1 boh3nya4 kah3win2. (He didn't actually get married./It's untrue that he got married.)
Ie1 e33 kong1wa33 boh3theng3-boh3theng2. (He can talk without stopping.)
Lu4 mm33thang3 an1nae1 boh3yong3. (Don't be so useless.)
Lu1-eh3 bor4 co1kang1 ta1lok1? (Where does your wife work?)

english: wood fungus, cloud ear fungus (Auricularia polytricha)
example: Cit1-leh1 thng1 uh33 haeh33 bork3nee4. (This soup has wood fungus.)
Wah1 ca33hnui1 tnui4 lai2. (I came back yesterday.)
Wah1 ka1 wah1-eh3 ca3bor1lang2 cham3seong2 liau4, wah1 ka1 ka3 lu1 kong4. (After I have discussed with my wife, I will let you know.)

english: (mythology) god of wealth, (figuratively) very wealthy man, treasurer

example: Cit1-leh1 can1keng1 si33 yong33 lai3 siu3 bee4. (This warehouse is used to store rice.)
Wah1 boh3 siang3sin3 Cap3-Pek1 Tay3gaik1. (I don't believe in the Eighteen Levels of Hell.)
Cit1-leh1 si33 cau33hnui1-eh3 po1cua4. (This is yesterday's newspaper.)

english: vegetarian diet (often adopted for religious reasons)

example: Ta33-ta33 jit1 ie1 than3-tiok3 ce33-ce33 lui1. (Everyday he earns a lot of money.)

english: Zhangzhou, a prefecture-level city in Fujian Province, China. As of 2010, it has a population of 4.8 million people. It is the site of the former seat of the Imperial Chinese Zhang Prefecture. It is located on the Jiulong River about 56km (35 miles) from central Xiamen. The dialect of Hokkien spoken in Zhangzhou is noted for being the base dialect for Penang Hokkien.
example: Ie1-eh3 cor1kong1 Cheang3chew1 lai2 eh2. (His ancestors came from Zhangzhou.)
Ee1 cheng33 cha3kiak1. (She wears wooden clogs.)
Wah1lang2 cham3kuan1 cit3-leh3 chniau4. (We made a tour of a factory.)

english: 15th night of Chinese New Year, regarded as Chinese Valentine's Day

example: To3long3 cheet1 tok1teng3. (Please wipe the table.)
Sin3 Cnia1 wah1lang2 cheng33 sin3 sna1. (We wear new clothes during Chinese New Year.)
Cit1-khuan4-eh3 hu3 cin3 ce33 chi3. (This type of fish has a lot of fish bones.)
Chia3lien4 tiok3boek1 uh33 ta1yar4 ba3lu1 e33 knia1. (The wheel of the car needs a tayar to go on the road.)
Mee3hoon4 chin3 liau4 boey3? (Have you weighed the flour?)

english: an unrefined term to mean useless, hopeless; 2. pistol; 3. masturbation (slang, figurative)
example: A33si33 hor33 ie1 phak3, tnia3tiok1 be33 tiok1 eh2, in3ui33 ie1 chiu1cheng3. (If you let him shoot, for sure he will miss the target.)
Ie1 beh4 hor33 ie1-eh3 bor4 cit3-tiau3 kim1-eh3 chiu1pio1. (He bought for his wife a gold watch.)
Ie1 a1boey33 chnae4 gok3. (He hasn't woke up yet.)
Ti3 si2 lu1 boek1 chnia1 kah3win3 tok3? (When are you going to have your wedding dinner?)

english: to utter (expletives), to swear, to curse, to hurl abuse
example: Mm33thang3 chok1 chor3wa33 ti1 cit1-peng2. (Do not utter expletives here.)

english: unruly person, a person of indelicate nature, labourer, blue-collar worker
example: Chor3lang2 ai1 yong33 chor3wa33. (Rough people like to use profanity.)

english: up to you, whatever, as you wish
example: Lu1 ai1 ciak3 ha1mik1, chut1cai3 lu1 keng4. (Whatever you want to eat, it's up to you to choose.)
Cit1-leh1 ko3seng1 cin3 chut1mia2. (This artiste is very famous.)
Ti1 Penang khan1khor1 khnua3 tiok3 chye1hnui2 liau4. (It's hard to find a vegetable garden in Penang.)
Lu1 yong33 ha1mik1 phiau1-eh3 chye1iu2? (What brand of vegetable oil do you use?)
Wah1 uh33 cit3-khien1 peng3iu4 co1 chye1kor1. (I have a friend who is a vegetarian nun.)
Cit1-leh1 si33 cui33-cui33 lang2-eh3 chye1nah2? (Whose vegetable basket is this?)
Yong33 chye1to1 lai3 chiet1 chye1. (Use a vegetable knife to cut the vegetables.)
Ee1-eh3 knia4 mai1 ciak3 chye3. (Her child does not want to eat vegetables.)

english: who, whom, whoever (alternative form of cui3cui3)

example: Ie1-eh3 chu3 cin3cnia1 tua33 keng1. (His house is very large.)
Ie1 cit3si1lang2 mm3bat1 ceh33 poay3ki1. (He has never taken the plane his whole life.)
Heh1-leh1 ciu1 au1 cin3cnia1 swee4. (That wine glass is very beautiful.)
Wah1-eh3 bor4 yong33 ciu1pneah4 co3 huat1 kuah4. (My wife uses yeast cake to make huat kuih.)
Wah1 boh3 ciak3 ciu4. (I don't drink alcohol.)
Cnae1cui4 cin3 leng4. (Well water is very cold.)
Tua3 ie1-eh3 chu3-eh3 au33boey4 uh33 cit3-leh3 cnae4. (Behind his house is a well.)
Cit1-tiau3 lor33 cnia1 tng2. (This road is very long.)
Lu1 co1 ha1mik1 kui4 ti1 cit1-peng2? (What are you doing here?)
Ha1mik1 su33 lu1-eh3 co1kang3lang2 kin3jit1 boh3 lai2? (Why doesn't your worker come today?)
Co1mik1 su33 lu4 mai1 lai2. (Why do you not want to come?)
Wah1 mm33bat1 khnua3 koay3 cui1chia1. (I have never seen a waterwheel.)
Lu1 bat1 khee3 Ang3mor3 hua3hnui2-eh3 cui1chiang2 boh2? (Have you been to the Penang Botanic Gardens waterfall?)
Tha1na1 boh3 ce33 lang2 uh33 cui1chng2 liau4. (Nowadays not many people have waterbeds.)
Cit1-peng2-eh3 cui1cit3 boh3 a1nae1 ho4. (The water quality here is not that good.)
Wah1 hien33tiok3 nor33-ciak1 cui1gu2 ti1 chan2. (I saw two water buffaloes in the paddy field.)
Ie1 choay33 cit1-leh1 kang4-eh3 cui1guan3thau2. (He is looking for the source of this river.)

Cit1-khien1 kang3lang2 lai3 siu3li4 wah1-eh3 cui1kong4. (This workman came to fix my water pipe.)
Lu1-eh3 por1tor4 tua33 siang3ka1 cui1kooi3! (Your stomach is as big as a water barrel.)

english: water gate, sluice gate; see also cui1cak1
example: Cit1-leh1 kang4 uh33 cui1mui2 hor33 cun2 e33 koay3. (This river has a water gate for ships to pass through.)

Ie1 e33 tua3 cui1te4 ku1-ku4. (He can stay underwater for a long time.)

Ie1 cheng33 nor33 en2-eh3 sna1. (He wears two layers of clothing.)
I love to eat water convolvulus fried with sambal.
kin3jit1-eh3 hai4 boh3 eng4. (Today the sea is calm (has no waves).)
Wah1 se1han3-eh3 si2, wah1-eh3 ma3ma1 uh33 chnia3 cit3-khien1 gin1na1kang1. (When I was small, my mother hired a young servant maid)

Heh1-leh1 gong33tua33tai1 co3 phnai4 wah1-eh3 mi3knia3. (That stupid fella broke my thing.)

Ha1mik1 si33 lu4-eh3 guan33bong33? (What is your wish?)
Wah1 ha1hee3 in3ui33 wah1 ai1khoon3 liau4. (I yawn because I am sleepy already.)

english: why, how come; 2. what's the matter
example: Ha1mik1 su33 lu1 lai2? (Why do you come?)
Kin3jit1-eh3 hai1eng4 cin3cnia1 kuan2. (The waves today are very tall.)
Ka1liau1 lang2 tiok3boek1 hap3cork3. (Everybody must unite.)

english: untrustworthy, unreliable, undependable, good for nothing, bullshit
example: Wah1 mai1 kio33 ie1 co3 mi3knia3 ho33 wah4 in3ui33 ie1 hau3lark1 eh2. (I won't ask him to do anything for me because he is undependable.)
Wah1 su3kak1 ciak3 hniau3kor1. (I like to eat Chinese shittake mushrooms.)
Knia2 ho1-ho4. (Walk carefully.)
Lu1 ho1si3 boh2? (Are you okay?)

Ti1 Penang boh3 hong3chia1 liau4. (There are no windmills in Penang anymore.)
Ha1mik1 khah1 yau1kin4, hu1kui3 a33si33 kien3khong1? (What is more important, wealth or health?)

english: to yell, to bawl, to howl; 2. to call out, to state
example: Ie1 luan33-luan33 huak1 kae1cni2. (He wildly quoted the prices.)
Ha1mik1 su33 lu1 huan3lo4? (Why are you worried?)

english: war, revolt, oppose (huan4 is the literary reading for peng4)

example: Cit1-leh1 kuih4 boh3 haeh33 huat1hoon4 eh2. (This kuih doesn't require baking powder.)
Wah1-eh3 io1 snui1. (My waist aches.)
Wah1 su3kak1 ciak3 iu3chye3. (I like to eat vegetable in oil.)

english: word, (alphabet) letter, character (especially a Chinese character)
example: Cit1-tniau3 phe1 uh33 koay1 nor33-cheng3 ji33. (This letter has over two thousand words.)
Cit1-liap3 nui33 uh33 nor33-liap3 jin2. (This egg has two yolks.)

english: (stomach) to become bloated; (fried food) to have lost its freshness
example: Wah1-eh3 por1tor4 jip3 hong1 liau4. (My stomach is bloated.)

Ee1-eh3 sna1 co3 ka1 yong2 eh2. (Her dress is made of wool.)

english: unrefined word to denote a person who is problematic, difficult and troublesome
example: Wah4 mai1 kau3 peng3iu4 ka1 ie1 in3ui33 ie1 cin3 ju3siau2. (I do not want to befriend him as he is problematic.)

example: Wah1 ka3 lu4 khee3. (I go with you.)

english: to upbring, to educate, to imbue with good manners, to raise a child

Mm33thang3 co1 kae3kang1. (Don't waste effort.)

english: very cunning, devilish, diabolical, treacherous, hypocritic, sly
example: Ie1 hor33 wah4 kan3sin2-eh3 chio3. (He gave me a devilish laugh.)

Kau1si2 lu3 ka1 hor33 ie1 cai1. (When the time comes, then you tell him.)
Wah1 thark1 kau3 ka1 cit1-peng2. (I read up to here.)

example: Cit1-teng1 chia1 boh3 kha4 beh4. (This car is not worth buying.)
Ie1 cin3 boh3eng2 co3 ie1-eh3 khang3khoay3. (He is very busy doing his work.)

example: Ie1 gau3 khau3 ca3bor4. (He's good at flirting with women.)

Heh1-leh1 chu1teng4 boek1 lap3 liau4. (The roof is about to collapse.)

english: wooded area, forest. Lim2 is the literary pronunciation of 林 which is otherwise pronounced as na2.

Wah1 pau1 heh1-leh1 mi3knia3 khee4 lai2. (I wrapped up the item.)

english: to wear a scarf or headgear
example: Heh1-leh1 pau3thau2-eh3 Huan3na1 ca3bor4 hnua33 chia1. (The Malay lady wears a headscarf is driving.)
Ie1-eh3 knia4-eh3 bat3ciu1 phak1ciau4. (His son's eyes are crossed.)

english: to work diligently, to strive, to work hard

example: Haeh33 cit3 nor33 en2-eh3 phak1tay1chat3. (Apply one or two layers of undercoat paint.)
Cit1-leh1 cha2 tiok3boek1 phak1tay4 cit3 en2-eh3 chat3. (This wood needs to be undercoated with a layer of paint.)
Ie1 au1boey4 pun1 tiok3 phak3to4. (In the end he was also defeated.)
Ie1 pheng3seong2 khi4 ca1-ca4. (He usually wakes up early.)
Pheng3seong3si2 wah1lang2 boh3 lai3 cit1-peng2 ciak1 eh2. (We usually don't come here for meals.)
Ie1-eh3 bin33 cin3 phnai1khnua3. (His face is so ugly.)
Ie1 co1 sna1-eh3 phoay1huat1seong1. (He is a clothing wholesaler.)
Cit1-peng2-eh3 tiam3 ka1liau4 si33 co3 phoay1huat3-eh3 seng3li4. (The shops here do wholesale business.)
Ie1 ta33-ta33 jit1 uh33 lim3 cit3-au1-eh3 phu3to3 ciu4. (He drinks a cup of wine every day.)
Phua1kaek3-eh3 lang2 gau3 sng4. Wasteful people waste a lot.

english: village centre, small town (from pekan, MYL)
example: Ba3 Le3pai4-eh3 po1kan1 cin3 se3. (The town centre of Bayan Lepas is very small.)
Kin3jit1 wah1-eh3 ma3ma1 co3 po3piah4. (Today my mother makes popiah.)
Wah1 be33hiau1 pun3 chui3. (I don't know how to whistle.)
Boh3 tatt1 chie33 put1hau3-eh3 knia4. (Not worth raising unfilial children.)
Ie1 tiok3 or3kong1 in3ui33 co1 sa3lah3. (He was fined for wrong doing.)
Kin3jit1 wah1lang2 co3 sai1yong3 chye3-eh3 thng1. (Today we made watercress soup.)
Uh33 cin3 ce33 sai1to1 ti1 cit1-peng2-eh3 hai4. (There are many wolf herrings in the sea here.)
Ie1 tiok3 phak1chniau4 boh3lang3 boek1 co3 ie1-eh3 sak1si3. (Nobody wanted to be the witness to his robbery.)
Lu1 boek1 kor3 ie1 sam3pai1 ti3si2? (How long do you want to continue taking care of him?)
Ciak3 pnui33 liau4 ie1 say1 thau3keong1. (Having eating, he washed his spoon.)
Wah1lang2-eh3 say1sna3ki1 phnai4. (Our washing machine broke down.)

english: (figurative, colloquial) to scrub, to give a dressing down, to rebuke
example: Ie1 hor33 tau3ke1 say4. (He was scolded by the boss.)
Wah1-eh3 se1han1ee2 ba3lu1 kah3win2. (My mother's youngest sister has just gotten married.)
Wah1-eh3 knia4 se1han3 gok3. (My son is still small.)
Ie1 si33 mm33 si33 se1kai3 te33-it1 ho1giak1-eh3 lang2? (Isn't he the world richest man?)
George Town si33 cit3-leh3 se1kai3 ui3san4. (George Town is a World Heritage site.)
Ee1 co3 see1kark1tau33 char1 sam3bai1. (She makes winged bean stir-fried in sambal.)

english: to wedge, to insert, to slip under or in between
example: Wah1 sek3 cit3-tniau3 cua4 jip3 wah1-eh3 te3ya4. (I slip a piece of paper into my pocket.)
Ceng1hu4 mai1 lang3 seong3teong33 yong33 cui4. (The government does not want people to waste water.)
Langkawi ti1 Malaysia-eh3 si3por33. (Langkawi is on the western part of Malaysia.)

english: to write; 2. to do desk work, to hold a clerical position
example: Ie1 co1 sia1ji33-eh3 kang1. (He holds a clerical job.)
Heh1-leh1 beng3seng1 siau1lien2-eh3 si2 cin3 en3tau2. (That movie star was very handsome when he was young.)
Siau1lien3kay1 tha1na1 te3ya4 pa1cat1. (Youngsters nowadays are quite loaded.)
Cui33-cui33 lang2 cng1 hor33 lu4 lu1-eh3 sin3niau3chia1? (Who decorated for you your wedding car?)
Uh33 cit3-leh3 sio1cia4 boek1 choay33 lu4. (There's a young lady looking for you.)
Cit1-peng2-eh3 piak3 sip1cui4. (The walls here show water seepage.)
Niau1 uh33 sit1 boh2? (Do cats have wings?)

english: warehousing; 2. to receive goods or inventory

example: Lu4-eh3 sna3tu2 cin3 cat1 liau4. (Your wardrobe is very full.)
Ha1mik1 si33 lu1-eh3 snaeh3sniau3? (What is your Zodiac sign?)
Mm33thang3 ka3nia33 lim3 sng3cui4. (Don't keep on drinking iced water.)
Mm33thang3 sng1 cua4. (Don't waste paper)
Ie1 sniah3-sniah1 ku1-ku3 kong4 ha1mik1? (What does he whisper?)
Wah1 kin3 ni2 cin3 soay3siau2. (I am so unlucky this year.)
Heh1-leh1 phnai1si4-eh3 gina1na4 hor33 ie1-eh3 pa3pa1 sut3. (The naughty boy was caned by his father.)
Lu1 tua3 ta1lok1? (Where do you live?)

english: to wear a hat (LTR for ti3)

example: Cit1-leh1 tua3la1 tam2. (This towel is wet.)
Wah1 kio33 wah1-eh3 sio1moi3-eh3 ang1 co1 tang3mui2. (I call my younger sister's husband as tang3mui2.)
Cit1-leh1 chia1 boh3 tat1 beh4. (This car is not worthwhile buying.)
Heh1-leh1 gin1na1knia4 boh3 cheng3 te1khor1. (The little boy was not wearing underpants.)

english: younger brother's wife; husband's younger brother's wife; sister-in-law
virtue, morality, ethics, favor, benevolence, kindness
example: Ie1-eh3 tau3ke1 ho1 teik3-eh3 lang2. (His boss is a person of good conduct.)

english: to walk off with, to steal away, to make away with; to run away, to flee
to wear hat (homophone for tee3, CLR of tai3)
example: Wah1 ti3 ie1 hor33 wah4-eh3 bo3. (I'm wearing the hat that he gave me.)
Lu1 ti3si2 pang1kae4? (When are you having your school holidays?)

english: to unknowingly offended a deity or wandering spirit and is thus punished with some form of illness.

example: Wah1 beh4 sin3 tnia4 ti1 cap3hoay1tiam4. (I bought a new wok from the sundry shop.)
Wah1-eh3 tniau33lang2 boh3 co1kang1 liau4. (My father-in-law is no longer working.)
Ie1-eh3 tniau33mm4 gau3 co1 lak1sa4. (His mother-in-law is good at preparing laksa.)
Ka1liau4-eh3 mi3knia1 ti1 ie1-eh3 pang3keng1 to1tiau3. (Everything in his room is upside down.)
Taiping uh33 cit3-leh3 tong3boot3hnui2. (Taiping has a zoo.)

english: Zhu Bajie, the pig in Journey to the West
example: Too3 Pat1 Kai3 cin3 tham3ciak1. (Too3 Pat1 Kai3 is very greedy.)

Cit1-leh1 ku33 chia1 kokh1 uh33 tat3 kae3 gok3. (This old car is still worth something.)
Cit1-khien1 lang3 cin3cnia1 uh33lui1. (This man is very wealthy.)
Cit1-leh1 mi3knia3 lu1 beh4 eh2 uh33yong33 boh2? (This thing that you buy, is it useful?)
Ee1 boek1 kah3 wah4 co1 wan3than1. (She wants to teach me to make wonton.)
Ie1 uh33 seng3li4 ti1 Wat3lam2 (He has business in Vietnam.)
Wah1 boh3 beng3peik1 ie1 kong4 ha1mik1 wna1ko1. (I don't understand whatever he is talking about.)
Cin3cnia1 yau1kin4 cai3 ie1 khee33 i3seng3kuan4. (It's very urgent to send him to the hospital.)
Heh1-leh1 khit1ciak3a4 kui3jit1 yiu3 kae33lor33. (The beggar wanders the streets the whole day.)
Ee1 cheng33 yong3 sna1. (She wears a woolen dress.)
Cit1-leh1 gin1na4, chie3 ie1 leow1 bee4! (This child, feeding him is a waste of rice!)

english: to utter bad words, to utter expletives
to utter bad words, to utter expletives

example: Wah1-eh3 hoon3sor3 kah1 ie1-eh3 hoon3sor3 cuak3 thni1 kah1 cuak3 tay3. (There is a vast difference between the marks I received and what he received.)
Wah1-eh3 por1tor4 thnia3 ka1 boek1 si4. (My stomach is aching badly)

english: you're welcome (in response to 'thank you'), don't worry about it, don't stand on ceremony

example: Lu1 boek3 tok1 cit3-leh2? (Which one do you want?)
